text: One person that made me feel the magic of coding. And I remember him going like 'Ajke ekta bombastic problem solve korbo'

In terms of teaching he's the GOAT without any doubt. But apart from the teaching part no one can match the gem of a human being sir is. Sir is such a kind soul that when his students didn't do well in an exam sir blames himself and tell 'maybe I did lack in teaching you guys'. But in reality it was fault of the students because we didn't study properly.
KING! GOAT
GOAT PURAI

The best human, mentor, teacher you will ever find in your BracU journey. The man with zero hater! Always ready to help you. You are unlucky if you don't get the chance to meet him & attend his class.
GOAT of all GOATS
Best CSE110 faculty
He is one of the best faculty in BRACU I have ever seen, he is a good human being also, he gives many instruction, which as a freshers I need most.
If you follow his lecture notes closely and ask questions upfront, his insights are genuinely deep. Very approachable on Discord — always responsive.
Moves too fast and relies heavily on slides without simplifying complex logic. Great personality outside class, but classroom delivery needs work.
Polarizing faculty. Brilliant person and super available, but teaching clarity is inconsistent. Works best if you are a self-learner.
Quizzes feel unnecessarily difficult and pacing is chaotic. Outside class he's great — Discord availability is 10/10. High variance experience.
Use his consultation hours and stay on top of the material and it becomes manageable. Not for passive learners at all.
Recent semesters have dropped significantly. Students from 230 are saying run — I would echo that. His past reputation does not reflect the current experience.
One of the hardest courses made harder by fast-paced teaching. Marking is inconsistent — can be lenient one exam, CG crusher the next.
Course notes are dense but valuable. His Discord support is the only reason I passed. Would not take again but respect the effort he puts in outside class.
Generally a good experience. Quizzes are easy but finals are a different beast. Build a good rapport early and she is very supportive.
Teaching is monotonous and speech-heavy. Clear favouritism in marking — if she likes you, grade bumps happen. If not, expect a tough time.
She was genuinely great with me — supportive, fair marks, and always willing to help in labs. Course load is very manageable.
High-risk high-reward. Her class is a split experience — some students rave, some walk away feeling targeted. Stay on her good side.
Labs are fine and she genuinely helps out during sessions. Lectures can drag but you cover what you need. Watch for inconsistency in marking.
Described as angel by some and CG destroyer by others — both are accurate depending on which side you fall on. Average teaching, politics-heavy marking.
Reads directly from slides and has a weak grasp of advanced Java. Her notes and past papers are excellent but class attendance barely helps.
Mocked a student for asking a question during consultation. Behavior is unacceptable. Below-average teaching. The only saving grace is the past paper collection.
Once praised as goated and pookie, but the last two semesters have been a completely different person. Volatile, mocking, and unpredictable. Proceed with extreme caution.
Run fly or die — the warning is real. Her resources are the best in the department for CSE220 but the emotional toll of her behavior is not worth it for most students.
For a first programming course, clarity is everything and she does not deliver it. Uses ChatGPT-generated code examples sometimes which confuses more than it helps.
Behavior is the biggest concern. Known to be dismissive of female students and harsh to anyone she perceives as not trying. Not a comfortable learning environment.
bro FFR is literally GOAT tier for CSE250. quizzes straight from slides, marking is insane, replies on discord at 2am ngl. 11/10 no cap
took FFR for 250 and never looked back. explains theory before jumping to circuits unlike most. assignments easy. the type of teacher who actually gives a damn. highly recommend
FFR is a gem fr. marking is so generous you'll be shocked. turned CSE250 from a nightmare into something enjoyable for me. angel behavior absolute 10/10
RAO's handnotes are literally all you need. covers everything from zero. quizzes are 4 questions where only 3 count so you get a free miss. 10/10 would take again
doesn't care if final answer is wrong, gives marks for correct steps. very partial mark friendly. dude is passionate about circuits ngl. GOAT fr
got me from failing midterm vibes to a solid A. his notes + discord availability is elite. never makes you feel stupid for asking dumb questions. top tier
TAV records his own videos for students lol who does that?? absolute pookie fr. bonus marks throughout semester literally saved my cgpa
asked him the same question 3 times bc i kept forgetting and he explained every time without getting annoyed. that's TAV for you. GOAT behavior 10/10
marking is so generous bro. wrote half blank on one question and still got marks somehow. bonus marks on top of that. script checking is a vibe ngl
AKDB's marking is just insane. students call it 100/10 and honestly that tracks. bonus ungraded assignments for extra marks while you actually learn. lab vivas no stress at all
first quiz was rough bc of time pressure but he fixed that in later ones. patient, answers on discord if you cant make morning consultation. solid choice for 250
been a lab fav for years. no bias, chill guy, personally calls students to check if they understood the material. CSE250 under AKDB was genuinely stress free
SDS is a teaching boss. 10/10 delivery, covers everything, no rushing. follows rubrics strictly so don't expect free marks. fair is fair. 4.0 is possible if you actually attend
one of the genuinely good human beings at bracu. treats you like a person not an attendance number. explains circuits in a way that just clicks. marking is honest no games
calling him legend is underselling it fr. strict marking but completely fair, partial marks where deserved. pookie behavior. one of the best in cse dep no cap
AQT is pookie and best of best no cap. lab vivas are stress free with her. marking is very lenient compared to stricter faculty for CSE250. behavior absolute 10/10
some say her theory lectures are average but honestly her marking makes up for everything. super comfortable to approach. one of the most student friendly in the cse dep
took CSE250 under AQT and zero regrets. doesn't make you feel stupid for basic questions. low pressure throughout. marking is lenient and fair tbh
NFS aka Need for Speed lol. pookie faculty fr. best in lab settings, patient with all queries, never makes you feel bad for not knowing something. highly recommend
Nafis Faisal is just a good guy. grading is fair, doesn't penalize for no reason, easy to talk to. been recommended by students for multiple semesters for a reason
best thing about NFS is the zero stress environment. explains clearly, patient with confusion, humble vibes. 250 and 350 under him was a good experience ngl
technically solid, hand notes are detailed. but bro asks random questions mid class and WILL scold you if you can't answer. not for everyone. high risk section
mixed experience honestly. speaks softly so hard to follow. no script review so you never know where you lost marks. proceed with caution fr
expects perfect calculations, little to no partial credit if final answer is wrong. strict classroom vibe. watch youtube before class or you'll struggle ngl
niaz sir is genuinely the pookie of CSE ngl. class atmosphere is zero stress, explains stuff without making you feel dumb. underrated gem fr
his marking is actually lenient af. even if your logic is like 10% correct he still throws partial credit. bro literally saved my cgpa
dont expect grade bumps at semester end tho, he says marks are already generous and tbh he's not wrong. fair guy
behavior top tier. sweetheart, approachable, giving QUZA vibes no cap. only thing is class can feel slow if you zone out so stay attentive
if you want a chill stress free CSE101 section pick NZRF blindfolded. gentleman mentor energy
ngl TNMF for theory is rough. lectures feel dry and half the info doesnt even match what comes in exams. got blindsided in finals after doing fine in mids
marking is pathetic fr. no partial, no grace marks, script recheck is a lost cause. inconsistent af too
she seems sweet in person sometimes but after finals good luck getting a mail reply. dismissive during script consultation too
lab section is kinda okay with her, but theory is a straight up run. foundation courses under her hit different and not in a good way
sabbir sir is a red flag for CSE101 bro. lectures feel like he is forced to teach. 3/10 teaching is being nice honestly
bro literally cant debug our code during consultation. we end up learning from batchmates and youtube recordings instead
always late to class we literally call him late boy. the dont care energy makes him super unapproachable for real
marking is decent-ish (like 7/10 maybe) but he's stingy with partial and projects get slashed without clear reason. no grade bumps either
nice person outside class maybe but for a cgpa sensitive course? risky pick fr. taking him is a gamble
Teaching is top-notch. He uses practical examples to make logic click — even confusing topics start to feel doable after a couple of classes.
Marking is genuinely generous. Got strong partial marks on quizzes where my logic was right but small things were off. Fair guy.
Honestly one of the kindest faculty in CSE. Patient, humble, never makes you feel stupid for asking basic questions in consultation.
Quizzes are standard to slightly hard but they prepare you really well for the midterm/final. Don't skip them.
Took CSE111 with him after CSE110 and it was equally good. Punctual, prepared, makes class engaging without being chaotic.
Funny warning: don't show up to consultation in Barcelona gear. He's a die-hard Real Madrid fan and the jokes never stop.
Strict about protocols (deadlines, formatting) but if you follow the rules everything is smooth. Never lost marks for stuff that wasn't my fault.
If you want a peaceful semester with good grades AND actually learn programming logic, take RKBM. No regrets.
Underrated faculty fr. Teaching is straight to the point — if you actually pay attention in class you don't even need YouTube tutorials.
Marking is fair and rubric-based but he's generous with partial credit. Got mark bumps during script viewing just by explaining my logic.
Took CSE111 with him too. Same vibe — efficient teaching, low-stress class, no GPA panic moments.
One important warning: zero tolerance for plagiarism. If you copy code in his section you will get caught and your grade will tank.
His CSE230 lectures are the clearest I've seen. Discrete math is hard but he breaks it down step by step.
Wish he solved a wider variety of edge-case problems but everything covered in class is gold. Quizzes follow the same patterns.
Behavior is genuinely 10/10. Polite, humble, never talks down to students. Approachable for grade-boundary discussions if you put in genuine effort.
Quizzes can be hard but they prepare you really well. By midterm you realize his quiz pattern is exactly what shows up on exams.
Legend. KNI builds your programming foundation from absolute zero with so much care. Tracing finally made sense in her class.
Behavior is angelic, like a second mom. Always kind, always patient, always punctual. Replies to emails fast.
Marking is strict but completely fair. You get exactly what you earned — no extra bonuses, no unfair deductions either.
BIG WARNING: quizzes start easy but get significantly harder after the mid-sem break. Don't get complacent in week 4.
Continued with her into CSE111. Same dedication, same fairness. If you actually want to learn programming and not just chase grades, take her.
Everyone I know who got A/A- in her section attended every single lecture. The ones who skipped struggled hard.
Coding ethics matter big time in her section. She catches plagiarism and won't tolerate it — easy way to ruin your semester.
Energetic and systematic. Live-codes everything in class. If you pay attention you actually learn programming, not just memorize syntax.
Step-by-step explanations are top-notch. He covers difficult codes that other faculty completely skip.
Marking is rule-based and fair. If your code works you get marks, if it doesn't you don't. Don't expect grace marks.
Real talk: he gets visibly annoyed if you're zoned out during lecture. Stay engaged or sit at the back quietly.
Quizzes are tricky on purpose — they're designed to prepare you for the midterm/final pressure. Practice his class files with a timer.
The people who did well in his section just took his words like gospel. If he says practice X, do it. It pays off in finals.
Friendly and humble outside class. The strict vibe is only when teaching — he wants you to actually get it.
Very student-friendly. Lucid voice, gentle teaching style. Foundation-friendly for CSE110 if you also do some self-study on the side.
Marking is the highlight. She's lenient, accepts late assignments without penalty in many cases. Generous on internal stuff.
Made hardware concepts in CSE230 actually digestible for me. Some find her style slow but I appreciated it.
Pre-exam review classes are gold. Her suggestions overlap heavily with actual questions — don't skip them.
CSE320 with her was the chillest semester. Behaved like a sweet older sister, very approachable for any concept doubts.
Slight warning: midterms/finals are strict-rubric. She doesn't bump grades by more than 1 mark even if you ask nicely.
Behavior is 10/10. Sweet, well-mannered, never raises voice. Just don't side-talk in class — that's her one trigger.
Heads up: punctuality and quiz mark release can be slow. You might not know your standing till close to finals.
Highly organized teaching. He doesn't just follow slides — he gives deeper insights that build your foundation properly.
Beginners can find his class overwhelming because of volume. If you're not committing time it'll feel like drinking from a firehose.
Marking has gotten stricter recently. He doesn't give grace marks unless you can actually defend your logic in script viewing.
If you want to actually master Data Structures, take AIB. His insights on recursion and trees are unmatched in the dept.
He values logic over effort. Wrong code = no marks even if you wrote 200 lines. Right logic with bugs = good partial.
Most student-friendly faculty in CSE no debate. Replies to emails at 1AM. Will help with internship references if you ask.
Mentor-level outside class. Spend 15 mins in consultation and you come out with career advice + course advice + life advice.
Not beginner-friendly — he assumes you already have some coding base. If you're starting from zero you'll need YouTube alongside his lectures.
Marking is the best part. Generous, bonus opportunities in quizzes, considers grace marks if you're at a grade boundary.
His explanations skip a lot of intermediate steps. Experienced coders will be fine, beginners struggle.
For CSE422L he's actually solid — at upper level his style works because everyone has the prereqs. Plus marking carries hard.
Best for research guidance and academic advice. If you want a thesis supervisor or internship reference, he's your guy.
Behavior is solid — supportive, humble, treats students with respect. Some complaints about phone use in class but overall a favorite.
Approachable on Slack/Discord for non-class stuff. Career advice is genuine and detailed.
